# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:2.7-slim-buster

# Install system packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    libpq-dev \
    libffi6 \
    libssl1.0.0 \
    libgdbm3 \
    libncurses5 \
    zlib1g \
    openssl

# Copy the requirements.txt file
COPY requirements.txt /app/

# Create app directory and set working directory
RUN mkdir -p /app
WORKDIR /app

# Install dependencies specified in requirements.txt
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy the application files
COPY . /app

# Expose port 8000 for our application
EXPOSE 8000

# Run the application when container starts
CMD ["python", "your_main_script.py"]
